basic_details.job_title: RSSB Computer Instructor Recruitment 2026: 3951 Vacancies - Apply Online by 23 June 2026
basic_details.short_information: The Rajasthan Staff Selection Board (RSSB) has announced Advertisement No. 07/2026 for the joint direct recruitment of Senior Computer Instructor and Basic Computer Instructor posts across the Secondary Education Department and Sanskrit Education Department. A total of 3,951 vacancies are on offer. Online applications open on 25 May 2026 and close on 23 June 2026 at 11:59 PM. The written exam for Senior Computer Instructor is proposed on 22 August 2026 and for Basic Computer Instructor on 23 August 2026.

application_fee.details: The fee for this recruitment is a One Time Registration (OTR) fee. Candidates who have already paid OTR in a previous recruitment are not required to pay again. | Candidates from outside Rajasthan belonging to SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS categories are treated as General category for this recruitment and must pay ₹600. | Payment can be made through authorised e-Mitra kiosks, Jan Suvidha Kendras (CSC), net banking, ATM-cum-debit card, or credit card. | Penalty for absenteeism: Candidates who do not appear in two exams conducted by RSSB/RPSC/any Rajasthan recruitment agency in a single financial year may have their OTR facility blocked. Restoring it the first time costs ₹750; a second block in the same financial year costs ₹1,500.

qualification.details: Candidates must verify eligibility against the prescribed educational qualifications before submitting their online application. Applying in the wrong category or with incorrect qualification details can lead to rejection at any stage of the recruitment process. | For Senior Computer Instructor: | Candidates must hold one of the following qualifications: | ME/M.Tech in Computer Science (CS) / Information Technology (IT) / Electronics & Communications Engineering (ECE) / Electrical Engineering (EE) / Electrical & Electronics Engineering (EEE) / Electronics & Telecommunication Engineering (ETE) / Electronics & Instrumentation Engineering (EIE) M.Sc. in Computer Science (CS) or Information Technology (IT) Master in Computer Application (MCA) / 'B' Level / 'C' Level Any equivalent or higher qualification recognised by the Government | For Basic Computer Instructor: | Candidates must hold one of the following: | Graduate with 'A' Level / PGDCA (minimum one year) BE/B.Tech in Computer Science (CS) / IT / ECE / EE / EEE / Electronic Instrumentation & Control (EIC) / Telecommunications & Instrumentation (TIE) B.Sc. in Computer Science (CS) or Information Technology (IT) Bachelor in Computer Application (BCA) from a university established by law in India Any equivalent or higher qualification recognised by the Government | Both posts additionally require working knowledge of Hindi written in Devanagari script and knowledge of Rajasthani culture. | Candidates appearing in the final year of the prescribed qualifying examination are also eligible to apply, but they must submit proof of completing the qualification before the written examination date. | Ex-servicemen who hold a certificate equivalent to graduation after 15 years of military service are also eligible to apply and may enter 'Not Applicable' in the roll number field and 'N' Grade in the result field during OTR. | Age is calculated as of 01 January 2027 (the first day of January following the application closing date). Candidates must be a minimum of 18 years and must not have completed 40 years as on 01 January 2027. | Important note for Secondary Education Department: All candidates who were age-eligible during the RSSB Senior and Basic Computer Instructor Direct Recruitment 2022 (where age was calculated as of 01-01-2023) are entitled to an additional 3 years of upper age relaxation for the Secondary Education Department posts only (since no recruitment occurred after that cycle). This additional relaxation does not apply to the Sanskrit Education Department posts, as this is their first recruitment. | Upper Age Relaxation: | Age relaxations are non-cumulative - candidates can claim only one of the above provisions. | DON'T MISSSSC CGL 12256 Group B & Group C Online Form 202612,256 Posts • Group B & Group C • SSC22 Jun 202612,256 postsRRB 6565 Technician Online Form 2026 (Short Notice)6,565 Vacancies • Technician29 Jul 20266,565 postsOSSSC 5989 Nursing Officer Online Form 20265,989 Openings • OSSSC13 Jul 20265,989 postsGSRTC 4599 Driver Online Form 20264,599 Posts • GSRTC05 Jul 20264,599 postsCentral Bank of India 4,500 Apprentice Recruitment 20264,500 Vacancies • Apprentice • Central Bank of India22 Jun 20264,500 postsGSRTC 4318 Conductor Online Form 20264,318 Openings • Conductor05 Jul 20264,318 posts

salary_details.details: The 7th Pay Commission pay matrix levels have been assigned for both posts. During the two-year probation period, candidates will receive only a fixed monthly remuneration at rates prescribed by the state government - no allowances such as HRA, DA, or City Compensatory Allowance are payable during probation. | After successful completion of probation - including training, passing the prescribed examination, and meeting integrity requirements - candidates become eligible for the full pay scale and other allowances as per service rules.

selection_process: The selection for both posts follows a computer-based / tablet-based written examination format. The Board may also conduct an OMR-based offline exam; the final mode will be confirmed through the official website. If more than one phase of examination is conducted
a normalisation process will be applied to determine final merit.
Both posts follow a two-paper pattern
with each paper carrying 100 marks (total 200 marks):
and Current Affairs of Rajasthan; General Ability (Logical Reasoning
Basic Numeracy at Class X level
Data Interpretation at Class X level) Negative Marking: 1/3 mark deducted per wrong answer Minimum qualifying marks: 40% (35% for SC/ST candidates)
2 hours duration Negative Marking: 1/3 mark deducted per wrong answer Minimum qualifying marks: 40% (35% for SC/ST candidates)
OMR Special Rule: Each question has five options (A
E). If a candidate leaves a question unattempted
they must darken option E. If more than 10% of questions have none of the five circles darkened
how_to_apply: Applications for RSSB Computer Instructor Recruitment 2026 are accepted exclusively online through the SSO Portal. No offline applications are accepted under any circumstances.
Visit https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in and log in with your SSO ID.
Under Citizen Apps (G2C), select the Recruitment Portal.

If you have not yet completed OTR (One Time Registration), go to the OTR tab, select your category (Unreserved or Reserved), enter your disability and home state details, and pay the applicable OTR fee.
After OTR is complete, click Apply Now for Advertisement No. 07/2026.
In the OTR Dashboard, carefully select your educational qualification in the Document Details section.

Upload required documents including educational certificates, category certificate, and sports certificate (if applicable under Outstanding Player quota) - all as per the formats and specifications published on the Board's website.
Fill in your department preference order (Secondary Education / Sanskrit Education) in the designated column. This cannot be changed later.
Enter your visible body mark (mandatory field).
Provide your own mobile number and email ID - all important communications will be sent to these contacts. Do not change them until the recruitment process is complete.
Review the complete application using the Checklist option under "My Recruitment" section before final submission.
Click Final Submit. An Application ID will be generated - save it for future reference.
Take a printout of the submitted application form and keep it safely.

Applications must be submitted by 23 June 2026 at 11:59 PM. After final submission, correction of name, father's name, date of birth, photograph, signature, thumb impression, and handwriting sample is not permitted. Other entries may be corrected within the window period or within 3 days after the application closes, subject to a correction fee of ₹300.
The photograph for this application is captured live via the OTR portal - no static image upload is allowed. Candidates must note the following to avoid rejection:
The live photo must be taken against a white or light-coloured background with the face covering at least 50% of the frame.
Head, eyes, nose, and chin must be clearly visible. No dark or tinted glasses; no cap or cloth covering the face.
The photo must not be blurred or dark.

The signature must be signed in a rectangular box of 7 cm × 2 cm on white A4 paper using a black or dark blue pen, then scanned and uploaded in JPEG format (size: 20 KB to 50 KB; minimum 280×80 pixels, maximum 560×160 pixels).
The left thumb impression must also be uploaded separately after the signature (right thumb if left is missing).
Mobile phone photographs of signatures are not accepted.

all_official_links.label: Download Official Notification - RSSB Computer Instructor Recruitment 2026

faq.question: Q1. What is the total number of vacancies in RSSB Computer Instructor Recruitment 20
faq.answer: There are 3,951 total vacancies - 322 for Senior Computer Instructor and 3,629 for Basic Computer Instructor — spread across the Secondary Education Department and the Sanskrit Education Department of Rajasthan.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply online for RSSB Computer Instructor 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 23 June 2026 at 11:59 PM through the SSO Portal at sso.rajasthan.gov.in. Applications submitted after this deadline will not be accepted under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for Senior Computer Instructor?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold an ME/M.Tech or M.Sc. in Computer Science/IT or a Master in Computer Application (MCA) or 'B'/'C' Level certificate, or any equivalent qualification recognised by the Government, along with working knowledge of Hindi in Devanagari script and Rajasthani culture.

faq.question: Q4. What is the qualification needed for Basic Computer Instructor?
faq.answer: A graduate degree with 'A' Level/PGDCA, or a BE/B.Tech in CS/IT/ECE/EE, or B.Sc. in CS/IT, or BCA from a recognised Indian university is required, along with knowledge of Hindi written in Devanagari script and Rajasthani culture.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary for Senior and Basic Computer Instructor posts?
faq.answer: The Senior Computer Instructor post carries Pay Matrix Level 10 and the Basic Computer Instructor post carries Pay Matrix Level 8 as per the 7th Pay Commission. During the two-year probation period, only a fixed monthly remuneration is payable - no additional allowances.

faq.question: Q6. When is the written examination scheduled for RSSB Computer Instructor 2026?
faq.answer: The exam for Senior Computer Instructor is proposed on 22 August 2026 and for Basic Computer Instructor on 23 August 2026. The Board reserves the right to change these dates; the final schedule will be notified on rssb.rajasthan.gov.in.

faq.question: Q7. How is the RSSB Computer Instructor exam structured?
faq.answer: The exam has two papers, each of 100 marks and 2 hours duration, totalling 200 marks. Paper I covers Rajasthan GK and General Ability. Paper II covers subject-specific computer topics. Negative marking of 1/3 mark applies for each wrong answer. Each question has 5 options (A to E), and unattempted questions must have option E darkened.

faq.question: Q8. What is the application fee for General and Reserved category candidates?
faq.answer: General category and creamy layer OBC/MBC candidates must pay an OTR fee of ₹600. Rajasthan-domiciled non-creamy layer OBC/MBC, EWS, SC, ST candidates, and all Divyang applicants pay ₹400. Candidates who have previously paid the OTR fee need not pay again.

faq.question: Q9. Is there any age relaxation for OBC and SC/ST candidates in this recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes. SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS male candidates who are Rajasthan domicile get 5 years of upper age relaxation. Women candidates from these categories (Rajasthan domicile) get 10 years. For the Secondary Education Department posts only, all candidates eligible during the 2022 recruitment cycle receive an additional 3 years of relaxation.

faq.question: Q10. Can candidates from other states apply under reserved category benefits?
faq.answer: No. Candidates originally from states other than Rajasthan who belong to SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS categories are treated as General category for this recruitment. They must pay the General category OTR fee and cannot claim reserved category benefits.

_backup_how_to_apply: Applications for RSSB Computer Instructor Recruitment 2026 are accepted exclusively online through the SSO Portal. No offline applications are accepted under any circumstances.
Visit https://sso.rajasthan.gov.in and log in with your SSO ID.
Under Citizen Apps (G2C), select the Recruitment Portal.

If you have not yet completed OTR (One Time Registration), go to the OTR tab, select your category (Unreserved or Reserved), enter your disability and home state details, and pay the applicable OTR fee.
After OTR is complete, click Apply Now for Advertisement No. 07/2026.
In the OTR Dashboard, carefully select your educational qualification in the Document Details section.

Upload required documents including educational certificates, category certificate, and sports certificate (if applicable under Outstanding Player quota) - all as per the formats and specifications published on the Board's website.
Fill in your department preference order (Secondary Education / Sanskrit Education) in the designated column. This cannot be changed later.
Enter your visible body mark (mandatory field).
Provide your own mobile number and email ID - all important communications will be sent to these contacts. Do not change them until the recruitment process is complete.
Review the complete application using the Checklist option under "My Recruitment" section before final submission.
Click Final Submit. An Application ID will be generated - save it for future reference.
Take a printout of the submitted application form and keep it safely.

Applications must be submitted by 23 June 2026 at 11:59 PM. After final submission, correction of name, father's name, date of birth, photograph, signature, thumb impression, and handwriting sample is not permitted. Other entries may be corrected within the window period or within 3 days after the application closes, subject to a correction fee of ₹300.
The photograph for this application is captured live via the OTR portal - no static image upload is allowed. Candidates must note the following to avoid rejection:
The live photo must be taken against a white or light-coloured background with the face covering at least 50% of the frame.
Head, eyes, nose, and chin must be clearly visible. No dark or tinted glasses; no cap or cloth covering the face.
The photo must not be blurred or dark.

The signature must be signed in a rectangular box of 7 cm × 2 cm on white A4 paper using a black or dark blue pen, then scanned and uploaded in JPEG format (size: 20 KB to 50 KB; minimum 280×80 pixels, maximum 560×160 pixels).
The left thumb impression must also be uploaded separately after the signature (right thumb if left is missing).
Mobile phone photographs of signatures are not accepted.
